steps.title: Identify the sides
steps.content: In a right triangle, $\tan\theta=\dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}}$. From the figure, the side opposite $x^\circ$ is 26 and the adjacent side is 7.

steps.title: Identify the answer
steps.content: So $\tan x^\circ = \frac{26}{7}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. The tangent of an acute angle in a right triangle is defined as the ratio of the length of the side opposite the angle to the length of the shorter side adjacent to the angle. In the triangle shown, the length of the side opposite the angle with measure $x^\circ$ is $26$ units and the length of the side adjacent to the angle with measure $x^\circ$ is $7$ units. Therefore, the value of $\tanx^\circ$ is $\frac{26}{7}$.
